summary: Do not ship: the authoritative live probe can both misclassify authentication failure as ruleset enforcement and expose trust-anchor credentials. The advertised App-rename path is also not wired end-to-end, and one current R-46 overclaim remains.
triage: review-gate SKILL.md rules govern: render EVERY finding as a full table row, the human decides each row (accept/reject/defer/question — free text), echo back before applying, max 2 rounds, append decisions.md and COMMIT it with this artifact.

findings.title: The authoritative probe neither authenticates safely nor proves a ruleset rejection
findings.body: B2 places the owner PAT in the URL's username position, although GitHub HTTPS authentication uses the PAT as the password. Credential-helper state can therefore make this prompt, fail authentication, or reuse another cached identity; any resulting nonzero push could be mistaken for the expected ruleset rejection. B2 and B3 also expose live tokens through command arguments and shell history, contradicting the later no-secret promise. Because Seam C is the accepted authoritative proof, this can falsely certify enforcement while leaking an admin credential. [GitHub PAT guidance](https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/managing-your-personal-access-tokens#using-a-personal-access-token-on-the-command-line)
findings.recommendation: Use an ephemeral GIT_ASKPASS or credential helper with tokens supplied outside argv/history, disable interactive fallback, and assert that failure is specifically a ruleset violation rather than authentication failure. Retain the exact writer-created ref through B4 and clean it up afterward.

findings.title: The documented App-rename override splits the ruleset identity from the executor identity
findings.body: The new configuration explicitly advertises writer_app_slug as the supported override after an App rename. The ruleset will then bypass the renamed App, but `.github/workflows/bump-poll.yaml:161-162` still commits with the old bot identity and `tools/ensure-bump-pr.ts:264,289` still trusts the old default slug. PRs authored under the renamed App are consequently classified as untrusted and the bump state machine fails closed. The only documented non-default configuration is therefore not wired end-to-end.
findings.recommendation: Either make the writer slug a shared configuration consumed by Terraform, the workflow git identity, and ensure-bump-pr's --writer argument, or remove the rename-support claim and fail validation when the slug differs from the fixed runtime identity.

findings.title: R-46 is still described as an F-0 residual in current tooling documentation
findings.body: The live tooling README still summarizes the force-push recheck as `TOCTOU 창 최소화 — 잔여는 F-0`, implying that F-0 is the remaining closure. That conflicts with the new invariant that F-0 only closes ref creation/push vectors and cannot eliminate concurrent PR creation. This file is current operational documentation, not one of the explicitly exempt historical bugfix artifacts.
findings.recommendation: Replace the phrase with the same explicit wording used in ensure-bump-pr.ts: F-0 narrows the exposure, while concurrent PR creation remains an accepted R-46 residual.

next_steps: Repair and re-review the Seam C authentication commands before merge.
Choose a single writer-App slug source or remove the unsupported rename path.
Correct the remaining current R-46 wording and refresh committed verification if executable procedures change.
